calendar.matters:
MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER / Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Proceedings
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER TARICK SHAMIYEH'S Motion To Bifurcate Proceedings.

Petitioner Tarick Shamiyeh's motion to bifurcate proceedings is granted in large part. Shamiyeh's request for judicial notice is granted in full. This writ proceeding will be divided into two phases. In the first phase the parties will address the applicable standard of review for Shamiyeh's administrative appeal and whether it was legal error for the ALJ to impose a burden of proof on Shamiyeh in the administrative appeal. All other issues, including whether Shamiyeh had any right to introduce non-record evidence beyond what is permitted by Rule 6B of the Rules for Administrative Appeal, will be addressed in the second phase, if any. Judicial economy is served by the foregoing bifurcation. The first phase issues are discrete and easily segregable from the second phase issues and necessarily must be decided prior to the second phase issues. The decision on those issues are possibly outcome-determinative. The first phase issues are subject of serious dispute, possibly novel, and involve pure issues of law.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Visting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
